texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the TREAT trial (9) , 4038 people with diabetes, chronic kidney disease not on dialysis, and anaemia, were randomly assigned in a 1:1 raTo to darbepoeTn α, to achieve Hb level of approximately 130 g/L or to placebo, with rescue darbepoeTn α when the haemoglobin level was less than 90 g/L. The primary end points were the composite outcomes of death or a cardiovascular event (nonfatal myocardial infarcTon, congesTve heart failure, stroke, or hospitalisaTon for myocardial ischaemia) and of death or end-stage kidney disease. AVer a median follow up of 29 months, there was no difference between the two arms in the primary outcome of death, cardiovascular event or end stage kidney disease. Fatal or nonfatal stroke occurred in 101 parTcipants assigned to darbepoeTn α and 53 parTcipants assigned to placebo (HR, 1.92; 95% CI: 1.38 to 2.68; p <0.001). The invesTgators concluded that for many involved in clinical decision making this risk of prescribing an ESA in this populaTon will outweigh the potenTal benefits (9) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Data from observaTonal studies have, however, not shown increased hazard risk among people who achieved higher Hb. In one study, data from people receiving haemodialysis in the UK Renal Registry from 1999 to 2005 were analysed for the relaTve risk of death at different Hb concentraTons. Hb concentraTons above the reference range (100-110 g/L) consistently showed a 35% lower relaTve risk of death, while people with haemoglobin below 100 g/L had a 28% higher mortality. The greatest mortality was seen in people with haemoglobin <90 g/L (73% increased risk of death, although due to the small numbers, this was not staTsTcally significant). On the other hand, the lowest death rate was seen in people with haemoglobin levels between 120 and 139 g/L (64% reduced mortality) (10) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of cumulaTve ESA dose was also reported in another retrospecTve study (11) . In this study, which looked at data from Medicare's end-stage kidney disease program between 1999 and 2007, different US dialysis centres annual anaemia management pracTce were characterised by esTmaTng their typical use of ESAs and intravenous iron in people receiving haemodialysis within 4 hematocrit categories. Monthly mortality rates were assessed using Cox proporTonal hazards regression to correlate centre-level paXerns of ESA and iron use with 1-year mortality risk in 269,717 people on incident haemodialysis. Monthly mortality rates were highest in people with haematocrit less than 30% (mortality, 2.1%) and lowest for those with haematocrit of 36% or higher (mortality, 0.7%). AVer adjustment for baseline case-mix differences, dialysis centres that used larger ESA doses in people with haematocrit less than 30% had lower mortality rates than centres that used smaller doses (highest vs. lowest dose group: HR, 0.94; 95% CI: 0.90-0.97). Centres that administered iron more frequently to people with haematocrit less than 33% also had lower mortality rates (highest vs. lowest quinTle, HR, 0.95; 95% CI: 0.91-0.98). texts.orig: Post Hoc analysis of the PIVOTAL Trial of 2141 people receiving dialysis, examining the baseline data, and potenTal relaTonship to the primary outcome (all-cause mortality, myocardial infarcTon, stroke, and heart failure hospitalisaTon), and associaTons with key baseline characterisTcs and QoL was measured using EQ5D index, Visual Analogue Scale (VAS), and the KD-QoL (Physical Component Score (PCS) and Mental Component Score (MCS)). The mean baseline EQ5D index and VAS scores were 0.68 and 60.7; 33.7 (PCS) and 46.0 (MCS). Female sex, higher BMI, diabetes mellitus, history of myocardial infarcTon, stroke or heart failure were associated with significantly worse EQ5D index and VAS. CRP and transferrin saturaTon (TSAT) were associated with QoL. Hemoglobin was not an independent predictor of QoL. TSAT was an independent predictor of PCS. CRP was associated with most aspects of QoL. Impaired funcTonal status was associated with mortality. Quality of life was impaired in people starTng hemodialysis. CRP was a consistent independent predictor of the majority of QoL scores. TSAT ≤20% was associated with worse physical component scores of QoL (12) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Several systemaTc reviews have looked at ESA or ESA and iron use, comparing various Hb target ranges and their effect on quality of life. One review by Spinowitz et al. looked at people receiving dialysis being treated with ESAs and without ESAs and its impact on their health-related quality of life (HRQoL). This showed that there may be some increase in HRQoL in those people treated with ESAs (with or without iron) vs those without treatment. However, there was no evidence for higher Hb targets leading to improved reporTng of HRQoL (13) .

One review by Spinowitz et al. looked at people receiving dialysis being treated with ESAs and without ESAs and its impact on their health-related quality of life (HRQoL). This showed that there may be some increase in HRQoL in those people treated with ESAs (with or without iron) vs those without treatment. However, there was no evidence for higher Hb targets leading to improved reporTng of HRQoL (13) .

However, there was no significant effect seen on physical funcTon or HRQoL (14) .

texts.orig: The findings of all the above studies have made it difficult to define a safe target Hb in CKD paTents treated with ESA. As a result, target Hb in this paTent group has been the subject of extensive debate in the literature:
texts.text: The findings of all the above studies have made it difficult to define a safe target Hb in CKD paTents treated with ESA. As a result, target Hb in this paTent group has been the subject of extensive debate in the literature:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · KDIGO suggests that for adults receiving dialysis, ESA therapy could be used to avoid having the Hb concentraTon fall below 90 g/L by starTng ESA therapy when the haemoglobin is between 90- 100 g/L (15) .
texts.text: KDIGO suggests that for adults receiving dialysis, ESA therapy could be used to avoid having the Hb concentraTon fall below 90 g/L by starTng ESA therapy when the haemoglobin is between 90- 100 g/L (15) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The Anaemia Working Group of ERBP expressed its view that Hb values of 110-120 g/L should be generally sought in the CKD populaTon without intenTonally exceeding 130 g/L In low-risk paTents (i.e., in younger paTents with very few comorbidiTes). In those with ischaemic heart disease with worsening ischaemic symptoms associated with anaemia, or in those in whom a clear benefit on quality of life can be foreseen, the start of ESA therapy could be considered at higher Hb values but not exceeding 120 g/L. In high-risk paTents, including those with asymptomaTc ischaemic heart disease, treatment iniTaTon with ESA should be started at Hb values between 90 and 100 g/L to maintain a Hb value ∼ 100 g/L during maintenance therapy (16) .
texts.text: The Anaemia Working Group of ERBP expressed its view that Hb values of 110-120 g/L should be generally sought in the CKD populaTon without intenTonally exceeding 130 g/L In low-risk paTents (i.e., in younger paTents with very few comorbidiTes). In those with ischaemic heart disease with worsening ischaemic symptoms associated with anaemia, or in those in whom a clear benefit on quality of life can be foreseen, the start of ESA therapy could be considered at higher Hb values but not exceeding 120 g/L. In high-risk paTents, including those with asymptomaTc ischaemic heart disease, treatment iniTaTon with ESA should be started at Hb values between 90 and 100 g/L to maintain a Hb value ∼ 100 g/L during maintenance therapy (16) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · NICE guidelines on managing anaemia in people with CKD suggest maintaining the 'aspiraTonal' Hb range between 100 and 120 g/L for adults (17) . The raTonale behind choosing a wide target Hb
texts.text: NICE guidelines on managing anaemia in people with CKD suggest maintaining the 'aspiraTonal' Hb range between 100 and 120 g/L for adults (17) . The raTonale behind choosing a wide target Hb

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is also suggested to consider accepTng Hb levels below the lower limit of the target range, if the target range cannot be achieved despite high, escalaTng doses of ESAs. High ESA doses are defined as: over 175 IU/kg per week for people receiving haemodialysis, over 125 IU/kg per week for peritoneal dialysis paTents and over 100IU/kg per week for pre-dialysis paTents (17) .

High ESA doses are defined as: over 175 IU/kg per week for people receiving haemodialysis, over 125 IU/kg per week for peritoneal dialysis paTents and over 100IU/kg per week for pre-dialysis paTents (17) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The health economics of anaemia therapy using ESAs has been subject to a NICE systemaTc review (17) which concludes that treaTng to a target Hb 100-120 g/L is cost effecTve in HD paTents (17) . Table 1 summarises the mean Hb data for prevalent UK dialysis paTents from the 24 th (2020) UK Renal Registry Reports (18) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) guidance (2007) notes that using ESAs to achieve Hb levels greater than 120 g/L is associated with an increased risk of death and serious cardiovascular events in people with CKD. The MHRA advises that Hb levels greater than this should be avoided, and that paTents should be monitored closely to ensure that the lowest approved dose of ESA is used to provide adequate control of the symptoms of anaemia. Use of ESAs to achieve Hb levels greater than 120 g/L is not consistent with UK markeTng authorisaTons for ESAs. Informed consent should be obtained and documented (19) .

texts.label: text

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Many studies have been published comparing different ESA products against each other when used at different dosing intervals, by different routes of administraTon and in different paTent groups. (1, 2) All the available products are efficacious when administered according to the manufacturers' recommendaTons. The choice of ESA will be dependent upon the clinician and paTent agreeing a management plan and local supply arrangements (2, 3) .

texts.text: Guideline 3.7 - Treatment of Anaemia - Ini9al ESA Dose:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest that the iniTal ESA dose should be determined by the individual's Hb level, the desired target Hb range, the observed rate of increase in Hb level and clinical circumstances. (2B)
texts.text: We suggest that the iniTal ESA dose should be determined by the individual's Hb level, the desired target Hb range, the observed rate of increase in Hb level and clinical circumstances. (2B)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For iniTaTon of ESA therapy, several points are to be considered:
texts.text: For iniTaTon of ESA therapy, several points are to be considered:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Type(s) of licensed ESAs available
texts.text: Type(s) of licensed ESAs available

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · IniTal ESA dose
texts.text: IniTal ESA dose

texts.label: list_item

texts.orig: · Route of ESA administraTon
texts.text: Route of ESA administraTon

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Frequency of ESA administraTon that best fit paTent requirements and achieve maximal convenience
texts.text: Frequency of ESA administraTon that best fit paTent requirements and achieve maximal convenience

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Monitoring for the anTcipated response in terms of Hb rise, rate of Hb rise, possible adverse effect (e.g. hypertension).
texts.text: Monitoring for the anTcipated response in terms of Hb rise, rate of Hb rise, possible adverse effect (e.g. hypertension).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In general, the aim of iniTal ESA therapy is to achieve a rate of increase in Hb levels of 10 to 20 g/L per month. This rate of rise is considered safe as evidenced from intervenTonal trials on people who are ESA naïve (1-3) . In CKD paTents with iniTal Hb levels less than target range, these trials have shown the mean iniTal rate of Hb level increase to be in the range of 7 to 25 g/L in the first 4 weeks. This rate of Hb increase is affected by the populaTon, iron status, iniTal ESA dose, and the frequency and route of ESA administraTon.

This rate of Hb increase is affected by the populaTon, iron status, iniTal ESA dose, and the frequency and route of ESA administraTon.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Guideline 3.8 - Treatment of Anaemia with ESA Therapy - Route of Administra9on:
texts.text: Guideline 3.8 - Treatment of Anaemia with ESA Therapy - Route of Administra9on:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest that the route of ESA administraTon should be determined by the CKD grade, paTent preference, treatment seLng, efficacy, safety, and class of ESA used. Subcutaneous (SC) use is preferable in people who are not receiving HD to avoid puncture of peripheral veins. Subcutaneous administraTon of short-acTng ESA preparaTons may be preferred in people receiving haemodialysis due to improved ESA efficacy at the EPO receptor, allowing for smaller doses to be used. However, convenience and choice may favour intravenous (IV) administraTon in people on HD. (2B)
texts.text: We suggest that the route of ESA administraTon should be determined by the CKD grade, paTent preference, treatment seLng, efficacy, safety, and class of ESA used. Subcutaneous (SC) use is preferable in people who are not receiving HD to avoid puncture of peripheral veins. Subcutaneous administraTon of short-acTng ESA preparaTons may be preferred in people receiving haemodialysis due to improved ESA efficacy at the EPO receptor, allowing for smaller doses to be used. However, convenience and choice may favour intravenous (IV) administraTon in people on HD. (2B)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the outpaTent seLng, SC administraTon is the only rouTnely feasible route of administraTon for non-HD CKD paTents. For HD paTents, either SC or IV administraTon is feasible. Among short-acTng ESAs, subcutaneous administraTon is associated with approximately 30% reducTon in dose requirements compared to that of IV administraTon for the same target Hb outcome. This has been proven in a large mulT-centre RCT on people on long term HD who had their haematocrit maintained within target range while on epoeTn α either via SC or IV route. ParTcipants were then randomised to IV or SC route. Upon randomisaTon, ESA doses were first decreased to allow haematocrit levels to decrease to less than target range. Doses were Ttrated upward to again achieve target haematocrit levels, and then were adjusted to maintain haematocrit in the target range during a 26-week maintenance phase. Among 107 people who completed the trial, those assigned to SC route showed 27% lower ESA doses than those assigned to IV administraTon (1) . However, not all parTcipants showed a dose decrease aVer conversion from IV to SC, and some paTents showed a dose increase. Among long-acTng agents, efficacy of SC administraTon appears to be equivalent to that of IV route at the examined dosing frequencies (2-5) .
texts.text: In the outpaTent seLng, SC administraTon is the only rouTnely feasible route of administraTon for non-HD CKD paTents. For HD paTents, either SC or IV administraTon is feasible. Among short-acTng ESAs, subcutaneous administraTon is associated with approximately 30% reducTon in dose requirements compared to that of IV administraTon for the same target Hb outcome. This has been proven in a large mulT-centre RCT on people on long term HD who had their haematocrit maintained within target range while on epoeTn α either via SC or IV route. ParTcipants were then randomised to IV or SC route. Upon randomisaTon, ESA doses were first decreased to allow haematocrit levels to decrease to less than target range. Doses were Ttrated upward to again achieve target haematocrit levels, and then were adjusted to maintain haematocrit in the target range during a 26-week maintenance phase. Among 107 people who completed the trial, those assigned to SC route showed 27% lower ESA doses than those assigned to IV administraTon (1) . However, not all parTcipants showed a dose decrease aVer conversion from IV to SC, and some paTents showed a dose increase. Among long-acTng agents, efficacy of SC administraTon appears to be equivalent to that of IV route at the examined dosing frequencies (2-5) .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Guideline 3.9 - Treatment of Anaemia with ESA Therapy - Frequency of Administra9on:
texts.text: Guideline 3.9 - Treatment of Anaemia with ESA Therapy - Frequency of Administra9on:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest that the frequency of administraTon should be determined by the CKD grade, preferences of treated populaTon, treatment seLng and class of ESA. Less frequent administraTon using long-acTng ESAs may be the treatment of choice in people with CKD not on haemodialysis. (2B)
texts.text: We suggest that the frequency of administraTon should be determined by the CKD grade, preferences of treated populaTon, treatment seLng and class of ESA. Less frequent administraTon using long-acTng ESAs may be the treatment of choice in people with CKD not on haemodialysis. (2B)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The frequency of ESA administraTon should be determined by the CKD treatment seLng and the class of ESA. Maximum efficacy is achieved by using the dosing intervals that are ESA class specific. In people receiving HD prescribed SC short-acTng ESA therapy, ESA efficacy is maximal when the drug is given thrice weekly. Short-acTng ESA efficacy decreases and dose requirement increases, when the dosing frequency is extended from thrice-weekly to once-weekly administraTon (1) . Increasing the Tme interval between dosages of long-acTng ESAs could also result in an increase in dose requirements (2) . Doses and dosing intervals should be tailored to each individual person's response to therapy. Use of algorithms may assist in opTmising therapy (3) .
texts.text: The frequency of ESA administraTon should be determined by the CKD treatment seLng and the class of ESA. Maximum efficacy is achieved by using the dosing intervals that are ESA class specific. In people receiving HD prescribed SC short-acTng ESA therapy, ESA efficacy is maximal when the drug is given thrice weekly. Short-acTng ESA efficacy decreases and dose requirement increases, when the dosing frequency is extended from thrice-weekly to once-weekly administraTon (1) . Increasing the Tme interval between dosages of long-acTng ESAs could also result in an increase in dose requirements (2) . Doses and dosing intervals should be tailored to each individual person's response to therapy. Use of algorithms may assist in opTmising therapy (3) .

texts.orig: We recommend that adjustments to ESA doses should be considered when Hb is <105 or >115 g/L in adults, young people and children aged 2 years and older. (1A)
texts.text: We recommend that adjustments to ESA doses should be considered when Hb is <105 or >115 g/L in adults, young people and children aged 2 years and older. (1A)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The NICE Guidelines for anaemia management in chronic kidney disease recommend an 'aspiraTonal' Hb of 100-120 g/L. It is anTcipated that if a populaTon Hb distribuTon is centred on this outcome with a mean of 110 g/L, then 85% of the populaTon will have Hb > 100 g/L (1) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In people receiving HD, withholding ESA doses for Hb levels greater than the target range is associated with subsequent downward Hb excursions oVen to levels less than target Range (2) . The Tme between withholding ESA doses and return of Hb to target range is variable and unpredictable. In HD paTents with Hb values greater than 140 g/L, the median Tme for Hb to return to 120 g/L or less aVer withholding of a SC-administered ESA is 7-9 weeks. The difference between withholding long and short acTng ESAs on the rate of Hb reducTon is not significant (3) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ESA dose adjustment may be higher during iniTaTon (or TtraTon aVer switching between different ESAs) than maintenance phases of ESA therapy. In a randomized double-blind trial comparing a short-acTng ESA with a long-acTng ESA in people on haemodialysis previously receiving epoeTn α, dose adjustments were made in 25% increments or decrements of the baseline dose, aiming to maintain individual Hb concentraTons within a range of 90 to 130 g/L (4) . Approximately 70% of paTents required dose adjustment in the 20-week TtraTon period, and 50% required dose adjustment during the 8-week maintenance period.

texts.orig: A post-hoc analysis of TREAT trial showed that: 7.4% of those with a history of malignancy at baseline died from cancer in the ESA arm compared to 0.6% in the placebo arm (P=0.002) (2) .
texts.text: A post-hoc analysis of TREAT trial showed that: 7.4% of those with a history of malignancy at baseline died from cancer in the ESA arm compared to 0.6% in the placebo arm (P=0.002) (2) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In a meta-analysis comparing possible adverse events related to ESA therapy, The higher Hb concentraTons in ESA treated people with NDD-CKD increased risk for stroke (RR 1.51, 95% CI: 1.032.21), hypertension (RR 1.67, 95% CI: 1.31-2.12), and vascular access thrombosis (RR 1.33; 95% CI: 1.16-1.53), and possibly the risk of death (RR 1.09; 95% CI: 0.99-1.20), serious cardiovascular events (RR 1.15, 95% CI: 0.98-1.33) or ESRD (RR 1.08; 95% CI: 0.97-1.20). (3) However, the risk of stroke was independent of Hb level or dose of ESA suggesTng other factors such as iron deficiency (4) .

(3) However, the risk of stroke was independent of Hb level or dose of ESA suggesTng other factors such as iron deficiency (4) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PaTents with neoplasia who received ESA in randomised clinical trials had an increased risk of tumor progression and reduced overall survival compared with study controls (5) .
texts.text: PaTents with neoplasia who received ESA in randomised clinical trials had an increased risk of tumor progression and reduced overall survival compared with study controls (5) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The MHRA advised that r-HuEPOs should not be given to people with cancer who do not fulfil the criteria in the authorised cancer indicaTons, and that paTents should be monitored closely to ensure that the lowest approved dose of r-HuEPO is used to adequately control of symptoms of anaemia (5) .
texts.text: The MHRA advised that r-HuEPOs should not be given to people with cancer who do not fulfil the criteria in the authorised cancer indicaTons, and that paTents should be monitored closely to ensure that the lowest approved dose of r-HuEPO is used to adequately control of symptoms of anaemia (5) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The joint guideline from the American Society of Clinical Oncology and the American Society of Haematology (6) recommends using ESA therapy with great cauTon in people with acTve malignancy, parTcularly when cure is the anTcipated outcome.
texts.text: The joint guideline from the American Society of Clinical Oncology and the American Society of Haematology (6) recommends using ESA therapy with great cauTon in people with acTve malignancy, parTcularly when cure is the anTcipated outcome.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NICE evaluated the efficacy and safety of ESA in treaTng anaemia in people with cancer receiving chemotherapy (7) . Although NICE researchers idenTfied 23 randomised controlled trials evaluaTng the effecTveness and safety of erythropoiesis-sTmulaTng agents (ESAs) for treaTng cancer treatment-related anaemia, NICE assessment focused only on trials that evaluated ESAs at a starTng dose reflecTng the current license (Hb <100 g/L). In total 16 studies were included in the analysis of the outcome related to anaemia and 7 trials in the outcome related to overall survival. NICE analysis of available trials concluded that erythropoiesis-sTmulaTng agents are recommended, within their markeTng authorisaTons, as opTons for treaTng anaemia in people with cancer who are having chemotherapy. ESAs were effecTve in increasing haemoglobin concentraTons, improving haematological responses, reducing the need for blood transfusions and improving health-related quality of life, but that it could not assume that ESA treatment either prolonged or shortened survival compared with treatment without an ESA (7) .

In total 16 studies were included in the analysis of the outcome related to anaemia and 7 trials in the outcome related to overall survival. NICE analysis of available trials concluded that erythropoiesis-sTmulaTng agents are recommended, within their markeTng authorisaTons, as opTons for treaTng anaemia in people with cancer who are having chemotherapy. ESAs were effecTve in increasing haemoglobin concentraTons, improving haematological responses, reducing the need for blood transfusions and improving health-related quality of life, but that it could not assume that ESA treatment either prolonged or shortened survival compared with treatment without an ESA (7) .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Guideline 3.13 - Monitoring of ESA Treatment - Haemoglobin during ESA Therapy:
texts.text: Guideline 3.13 - Monitoring of ESA Treatment - Haemoglobin during ESA Therapy:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest that Hb concentraTon should be monitored every 2-4 weeks in the correcTon phase or aVer a dose adjustment and every 1-3 months for stable individuals in the maintenance phase of ESA treatment. More frequent monitoring will depend on clinical circumstances. (2B)
texts.text: We suggest that Hb concentraTon should be monitored every 2-4 weeks in the correcTon phase or aVer a dose adjustment and every 1-3 months for stable individuals in the maintenance phase of ESA treatment. More frequent monitoring will depend on clinical circumstances. (2B)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is important to closely monitor Hb response to treatment to monitor for possible adverse events and plan ESA dose modificaTon. More frequent Hb monitoring may be needed for people with unstable Hb, out of target Hb level, anTcipated Hb drop due to blood loss/haemolysis, infecTon or subopTmal dialysis. The response to ESA therapy varies widely between different paTent groups and individuals within those groups. In addiTon, an individual's response can vary greatly dependent on other clinical variables. During ESA iniTaTon therapy, aVer drug dose adjustments or changes in an individual's clinical condiTon, more frequent monitoring is advised in order that under-treatment (ongoing anaemia) and overtreatment (rapidly rising Hb/hypertension or polycythaemia) may be avoided (1-3) .
texts.text: It is important to closely monitor Hb response to treatment to monitor for possible adverse events and plan ESA dose modificaTon. More frequent Hb monitoring may be needed for people with unstable Hb, out of target Hb level, anTcipated Hb drop due to blood loss/haemolysis, infecTon or subopTmal dialysis. texts.orig: Guideline 3.14 - Monitoring of ESA Treatment - Resistance to ESA Therapy:
texts.text: Guideline 3.14 - Monitoring of ESA Treatment - Resistance to ESA Therapy:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We recommend that inadequate response ('resistance') to ESA therapy is defined as failure to reach the target Hb range despite SC epoeTn dose >300 IU/kg/week (450 IU/kg/week IV epoeTn), or darbepoeTn dose >1.5 mcg/kg/week, or equivalent dose of methoxy ethylene glycol epoeTn beta following invesTgaTon and treatment of other causes. (1A)
texts.text: We recommend that inadequate response ('resistance') to ESA therapy is defined as failure to reach the target Hb range despite SC epoeTn dose >300 IU/kg/week (450 IU/kg/week IV epoeTn), or darbepoeTn dose >1.5 mcg/kg/week, or equivalent dose of methoxy ethylene glycol epoeTn beta following invesTgaTon and treatment of other causes. (1A)

texts.orig: Guideline 3.14.1
texts.text: Guideline 3.14.1

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest clinicians consider accepTng lower aspiraTonal haemoglobin target range in those on high, escalaTng ESA doses with inadequate response or consider alternaTve therapy such as a trial use of HIF-PHI for non-dialysis dependent CKD (NDD-CKD). (2C)
texts.text: We suggest clinicians consider accepTng lower aspiraTonal haemoglobin target range in those on high, escalaTng ESA doses with inadequate response or consider alternaTve therapy such as a trial use of HIF-PHI for non-dialysis dependent CKD (NDD-CKD). (2C)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Extensive publicaTons are available on the topic of resistance to ESA therapy including the Revised European Best PracTce Guidelines (1) which defines ESA resistance as above. Failure to respond at an earlier stage in therapy should however raise suspicion of ESA resistance.
texts.text: Extensive publicaTons are available on the topic of resistance to ESA therapy including the Revised European Best PracTce Guidelines (1) which defines ESA resistance as above. Failure to respond at an earlier stage in therapy should however raise suspicion of ESA resistance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Comparison of the individual Hb outcome achieved and the dose of ESA used can provide a useful way of highlighTng individuals that are ESA resistant during local unit audit (2, 3) . ESA therapy is efficacious in most people. However, many condiTons and treatment variables can cause or explain apparent resistance to ESA therapy. Adequate invesTgaTon and management of these underlying condiTons is crucial in achieving saTsfactory outcome haemoglobin values as well as requiring therapy in their own right.

texts.orig: PRCA is specifically characterized by the following clinical features (6) :
texts.text: PRCA is specifically characterized by the following clinical features (6) :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · A drop in Hb level of >7 to 10 g/L per week without transfusions or transfusion requirement of at least one unit per week to maintain adequate Hb, despite conTnued use of ESA at high doses.
texts.text: A drop in Hb level of >7 to 10 g/L per week without transfusions or transfusion requirement of at least one unit per week to maintain adequate Hb, despite conTnued use of ESA at high doses.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Markedly reduced reTculocyte count (<10,000 /uL).
texts.text: Markedly reduced reTculocyte count (<10,000 /uL).

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The most validated test is the radioimmunoprecipitation assay. This test is very sensitive and can detect the presence of high-affinity anti-EPO IgG at concentrations as low as 10 ng/ml. This test is not currently available within NHS but can be organised with the industry as a part of post-marketing pharmacovigilance arrangement with the PRCA Rare Disease Group.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ESA induced PRCA is an immune mediated process. While spontaneous remissions aVer cessaTon of EPO therapy have been reported, immunosuppressive therapy is usually needed in most cases (10) . One study evaluated 170 CKD paTents who developed epoeTn-associated PRCA (11) . Of the 34 people who received epoeTn aVer the onset of PRCA, 56% recovered epoeTn responsiveness; the highest rate of epoeTn responsiveness was observed among those who had no detectable anTerythropoieTn anTbodies at the Tme of epoeTn administraTon (89%). The study also reported that the highest recovery rates were among those treated with immunosuppressive therapy, parTcularly a combinaTon of cyclophosphamide and prednisone (11) . Other opTons such as rituximab, danazol or even plasma exchange may be considered.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Guideline 3.16 - Monitoring of ESA treatment - Hypertension during ESA therapy:
texts.text: Guideline 3.16 - Monitoring of ESA treatment - Hypertension during ESA therapy:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We recommend that blood pressure should be monitored in all people receiving ESAs and, if present, hypertension be treated by volume removal and/or hypotensive drugs. (1A)
texts.text: We recommend that blood pressure should be monitored in all people receiving ESAs and, if present, hypertension be treated by volume removal and/or hypotensive drugs. (1A)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hypertension is the most common complicaTon in CKD and can be aggravated by ESA treatment (1) . Earlier studies demonstrated higher incidence rates of hypertension though ESA doses used were higher and Hb responses faster in these trials. It is now more common to start at low doses and increase gradually according to response. The commonest cause of hypertension in CKD is not ESA therapy.
texts.text: Hypertension is the most common complicaTon in CKD and can be aggravated by ESA treatment (1) . Earlier studies demonstrated higher incidence rates of hypertension though ESA doses used were higher and Hb responses faster in these trials. It is now more common to start at low doses and increase gradually according to response. The commonest cause of hypertension in CKD is not ESA therapy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ExacerbaTon of hypertension in paTent on ESA therapy may be associated with polycythaemia or rapidly rising haemoglobin levels. These complicaTons should be looked for in paTents with hypertension but in the absence of these complicaTng factors and in the absence of severe hypertension, ESA therapy can usually conTnue. Hypertension should be adequately controlled prior to iniTaTng ESA therapy. ESA therapy should be disconTnued in malignant hypertension.

texts.orig: Guideline 4.1 - Treatment of Anaemia - HIF-PHI Agents
texts.text: Guideline 4.1 - Treatment of Anaemia - HIF-PHI Agents

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We recommend that treatment with HIF-PHI agents should be offered aVer iron repleTon, to people with symptomaTc anaemia (Hb <105 g/L) of CKD (stages 3-5 (eGFR <60 ml/min/1.73m 2 )) who are not receiving dialysis at the start of therapy and who are likely to benefit in terms of quality of life and physical funcTon and to avoid blood transfusion; especially in people considered suitable for transplantaTon. (1B)

(2B)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest, when deciding between ESA and HIF-PHI therapy for people with non-dialysis dependent CKD or DD-CKD, considering the preference of the person with anaemia of CKD, or, where appropriate, their family or carers, the cost of local drug supply, nursing and administraTon costs and previous treatment with ESA or HIF-PHI. (2B)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hypoxia-inducible factor-prolyl hydroxylase inhibitors (HIF-PHI therapy) also known as HIF stabilisers have emerged as a groundbreaking class of compounds that play a pivotal role in regulaTng various cellular processes. These oxygen-responsive heterodimeric transcripTon factors, known as HIFs (hypoxia-inducible factors), are integral to the cellular response to hypoxia, orchestraTng a cascade of events that influence vital physiological funcTons (1) . HIFs are parTcularly noteworthy for their role in managing oxygen homeostasis through their involvement in the producTon of endogenous erythropoieTn (EPO) and iron regulaTon within the body (1-3) .
texts.text: Hypoxia-inducible factor-prolyl hydroxylase inhibitors (HIF-PHI therapy) also known as HIF stabilisers have emerged as a groundbreaking class of compounds that play a pivotal role in regulaTng various cellular processes. These oxygen-responsive heterodimeric transcripTon factors, known as HIFs (hypoxia-inducible factors), are integral to the cellular response to hypoxia, orchestraTng a cascade of events that influence vital physiological funcTons (1) . HIFs are parTcularly noteworthy for their role in managing oxygen homeostasis through their involvement in the producTon of endogenous erythropoieTn (EPO) and iron regulaTon within the body (1-3) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Under hypoxic condiTons, such as those encountered at high alTtudes or in cases of anaemia, HIFs acTvate the transcripTon of EPO genes within the kidneys and liver, promoTng erythropoiesis to enhance oxygen delivery to Tssues (1-2) . AddiTonally, HIFs mediate the expression of genes involved in iron transport, uptake, and absorpTon. These genes contribute to the efficient uTlisaTon of iron, a vital element for mulTple physiological processes including oxygen transport, extracTon, energy producTon, and DNA synthesis (1-3) .
texts.text: Under hypoxic condiTons, such as those encountered at high alTtudes or in cases of anaemia, HIFs acTvate the transcripTon of EPO genes within the kidneys and liver, promoTng erythropoiesis to enhance oxygen delivery to Tssues (1-2) . AddiTonally, HIFs mediate the expression of genes involved in iron transport, uptake, and absorpTon. These genes contribute to the efficient uTlisaTon of iron, a vital element for mulTple physiological processes including oxygen transport, extracTon, energy producTon, and DNA synthesis (1-3) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Erythroferrone (ERFE) is the main erythroid regulator of hepcidin. When erythropoieTn is released from the kidney it sTmulates the producTon of new red blood cells and also increases the synthesis of ERFE in bone marrow erythroblasts. Increased ERFE then suppresses hepcidin synthesis, thereby mobilizing cellular iron stores for use in heme and haemoglobin synthesis. The mechanism by which hepcidin is decreased by HIF-PHI therapy has been aXributed primarily to increased producTon of erythroferrone due to increased haematopoiesis (4) . Hepcidin is a pepTde hormone that modulates the levels of ferroporTn - an iron-export channel found on the surface of cells in the duodenum, liver, and macrophages. Hepcidin's inhibitory acTon on ferroporTn suppresses both intesTnal iron absorpTon and iron release from macrophages, thereby influencing the overall iron balance in the body (5) . The connecTon between HIFs and hepcidin is significant; systemic acTvaTon of HIFs prompts an increase in endogenous EPO producTon and iron uTlisaTon by erythroblasts. This intricate interacTon subsequently downregulates hepcidin producTon in the liver, promoTng enhanced intesTnal iron absorpTon and mobilisaTon from iron stores (6-9) . This orchestrated interplay forms a sophisTcated regulatory network that maintains iron homeostasis, ensuring opTmal funcToning of various biological processes.
texts.text: Erythroferrone (ERFE) is the main erythroid regulator of hepcidin. When erythropoieTn is released from the kidney it sTmulates the producTon of new red blood cells and also increases the synthesis of ERFE in bone marrow erythroblasts. Increased ERFE then suppresses hepcidin synthesis, thereby mobilizing cellular iron stores for use in heme and haemoglobin synthesis. The mechanism by which hepcidin is decreased by HIF-PHI therapy has been aXributed primarily to increased producTon of erythroferrone due to increased haematopoiesis (4) . Hepcidin is a pepTde hormone that modulates the levels of ferroporTn - an iron-export channel found on the surface of cells in the duodenum, liver, and macrophages. Hepcidin's inhibitory acTon on ferroporTn suppresses both intesTnal iron absorpTon and iron release from macrophages, thereby influencing the overall iron balance in the body (5) . The connecTon between HIFs and hepcidin is significant; systemic acTvaTon of HIFs prompts an increase in endogenous EPO producTon and iron uTlisaTon by erythroblasts. This intricate interacTon subsequently downregulates hepcidin producTon in the liver, promoTng enhanced intesTnal iron absorpTon and mobilisaTon from iron stores (6-9) . This orchestrated interplay forms a sophisTcated regulatory network that maintains iron homeostasis, ensuring opTmal funcToning of various biological processes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the presence of oxygen, prolyl hydroxylase enzymes hydroxylate the oxygen-regulated HIF-α subunit, thereby targeTng it for proteasomal degradaTon (10) . HIF-PHI therapy sTmulates erythropoiesis primarily by upregulaTng endogenous EPO producTon, thereby promoTng red blood cell producTon and amelioraTng anaemia. Clinical studies have consistently demonstrated the efficacy and safety of HIF-PHI therapy in addressing the anaemia associated with CKD (11-14) .
texts.text: In the presence of oxygen, prolyl hydroxylase enzymes hydroxylate the oxygen-regulated HIF-α subunit, thereby targeTng it for proteasomal degradaTon (10) . HIF-PHI therapy sTmulates erythropoiesis primarily by upregulaTng endogenous EPO producTon, thereby promoTng red blood cell producTon and amelioraTng anaemia. Clinical studies have consistently demonstrated the efficacy and safety of HIF-PHI therapy in addressing the anaemia associated with CKD (11-14) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When there is a reducTon of blood oxygen levels, prolyl hydroxylaTon and degradaTon of HIF-α subunits are inhibited, resulTng in its cellular accumulaTon and dimerisaTon with the HIF-β subunits and this heterodimer acts as the transcripTon factor for the EPO gene (15-16) . Prolyl hydroxylaTon can be pharmacologically inhibited by oral HIF-PHI therapy (17-18) , which sTmulates erythropoiesis, largely by increasing endogenous EPO producTon from increased transcripTon of EPO genes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Phase three trials have confirmed the efficacy and safety of this class of drug. At the Tme of wriTng, only Roxadustat is available and approved in the UK and should be considered in people with CKD not on dialysis but can be conTnued when the person progresses to dialysis therapy based on NICE guidance. The drug can be used in people receiving dialysis according to the SmPC but this has not been reviewed by NICE for this purpose (from increased transcripTon of EPO genes).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Health related quality of life in the trials to date have given variable results. Some trials have suggested improvement in Health-related quality of life in comparison to ESA therapy but these are confounded by changes in haemoglobin concentraTons (19, 20) . Others suggest no significant change in health-related quality of life measures or funcTonal health scores (10-13) .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale for Treatment with HIF-PHI Agents (Guidelines 4.1-4.9)
texts.text: Rationale for Treatment with HIF-PHI Agents (Guidelines 4.1-4.9)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Published trials confirm that the six HIF-PHI agents with data are superior to placebo and noninferior to ESAs in increasing and maintaining haemoglobin (Hb) concentraTon among people with NDD-CKD and DD-CKD (1-33) . Large, randomised trials have demonstrated that Roxadustat (8-19) , Vadadustat (20-23) , and Daprodustat (1-7) are superior to placebo and/or non-inferior to ESAs in correcTng and/or maintaining Hb at target levels in NDD-CKD and incident and prevalent people with DD-CKD. These findings have also been seen with Molidustat, Enarodustat, and Desidustat (24-33) . The Hb response when using HIF-PHI therapy is dose-dependent and there are variaTons depending on the molecule used and trial protocol. Some molecules depending on the starTng doses used, increased the Hb more rapidly than others. Rates of blood transfusion are similar among paTents receiving HIF-PHI therapy versus ESA therapy and lower than among those receiving placebo in randomised trials.
texts.text: Published trials confirm that the six HIF-PHI agents with data are superior to placebo and noninferior to ESAs in increasing and maintaining haemoglobin (Hb) concentraTon among people with NDD-CKD and DD-CKD (1-33) . Large, randomised trials have demonstrated that Roxadustat (8-19) , Vadadustat (20-23) , and Daprodustat (1-7) are superior to placebo and/or non-inferior to ESAs in correcTng and/or maintaining Hb at target levels in NDD-CKD and incident and prevalent people with DD-CKD. These findings have also been seen with Molidustat, Enarodustat, and Desidustat (24-33) . The Hb response when using HIF-PHI therapy is dose-dependent and there are variaTons depending on the molecule used and trial protocol. Some molecules depending on the starTng doses used, increased the Hb more rapidly than others. Rates of blood transfusion are similar among paTents receiving HIF-PHI therapy versus ESA therapy and lower than among those receiving placebo in randomised trials.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Several meta-analyses have confirmed the clinical findings from these RCTs (34-40) . A recent metaanalysis of the phase 3 RCTS comparing HIF-PHI therapy to acTve comparator has confirmed the efficacy of this class of drug in both people on dialysis (both peritoneal and haemodialysis) and not on dialysis (41) . A total of 26 trials were included in this recent analysis with over 24, 000 parTcipants. The average length of follow-up was 16.5 months (range 6-42 months) and the mean baseline eGFR ranged from 15.9-22.1 ml/min/1,73m 2 . The mean age was 67.1 years (range 48-72 years). The authors found a staTsTcally higher change in Hb levels from baseline between HIF-PHI therapy at the dose selected compared to ESA of 1.0 g/L (95% CI 0.2-1.7) favouring HIF-PHI use but perhaps not clinically significant. There was no difference in those reaching the pre-specified Hb target (OR 1.04; 95% CI 0.88-1.22) (41) .
texts.text: Several meta-analyses have confirmed the clinical findings from these RCTs (34-40) . A recent metaanalysis of the phase 3 RCTS comparing HIF-PHI therapy to acTve comparator has confirmed the efficacy of this class of drug in both people on dialysis (both peritoneal and haemodialysis) and not on dialysis (41) . A total of 26 trials were included in this recent analysis with over 24, 000 parTcipants. The average length of follow-up was 16.5 months (range 6-42 months) and the mean baseline eGFR ranged from 15.9-22.1 ml/min/1,73m 2 . The mean age was 67.1 years (range 48-72 years). The authors found a staTsTcally higher change in Hb levels from baseline between HIF-PHI therapy at the dose selected compared to ESA of 1.0 g/L (95% CI 0.2-1.7) favouring HIF-PHI use but perhaps not clinically significant. There was no difference in those reaching the pre-specified Hb target (OR 1.04; 95% CI 0.88-1.22) (41) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Roxadustat, the first in class HIF-PHI to receive NICE guidance and SmPC recommendaTon has a halflife of around 15 hours in people with CKD, is orally bioavailable, leads to an increase in EPO levels aVer oral administraTon and lasts for up to 48 hours post-dose administraTon. Its efficacy and safety have been examined in eight randomised controlled trials involving 9600 paTents (8-19) .
texts.text: Roxadustat, the first in class HIF-PHI to receive NICE guidance and SmPC recommendaTon has a halflife of around 15 hours in people with CKD, is orally bioavailable, leads to an increase in EPO levels aVer oral administraTon and lasts for up to 48 hours post-dose administraTon. Its efficacy and safety have been examined in eight randomised controlled trials involving 9600 paTents (8-19) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The results of trials that included people treated with haemodialysis (HD) and peritoneal dialysis (PD) and single-arm trials among paTents treated with PD (19) have shown that HIF-PHI therapy is at least as effecTve among those receiving PD versus HD.
texts.text: The results of trials that included people treated with haemodialysis (HD) and peritoneal dialysis (PD) and single-arm trials among paTents treated with PD (19) have shown that HIF-PHI therapy is at least as effecTve among those receiving PD versus HD.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rationale
texts.text: Rationale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Current targets which aim for parTal correcTon of Hb are based on clinical trials conducted more than a decade ago (1-3) . These landmark trials compared higher versus lower Hb targets achieved using ESAs, in which major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE), mortality, and thromboTc events were more common among people assigned to the higher of the Hb targets (1-3) . In addiTon, one trial comparing a high Hb target with placebo (and a conservaTve rescue strategy) in people with NDDCKD and diabetes found an increased rate of strokes (4) . The current HIF-PHI trials compared with acTve comparator used standard Hb targets based on ESA therapy. These varied depending on the country with lower targets in the USA compared to Europe and the UK. No HIF-PHI trials to date involving people in the UK or Europe have compared Hb normalisaTon or near-normalisaTon with the currently recommended lower Hb targets for people with CKD.
texts.text: Current targets which aim for parTal correcTon of Hb are based on clinical trials conducted more than a decade ago (1-3) . These landmark trials compared higher versus lower Hb targets achieved using ESAs, in which major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE), mortality, and thromboTc events were more common among people assigned to the higher of the Hb targets (1-3) . In addiTon, one trial comparing a high Hb target with placebo (and a conservaTve rescue strategy) in people with NDDCKD and diabetes found an increased rate of strokes (4) . The current HIF-PHI trials compared with acTve comparator used standard Hb targets based on ESA therapy. These varied depending on the country with lower targets in the USA compared to Europe and the UK. No HIF-PHI trials to date involving people in the UK or Europe have compared Hb normalisaTon or near-normalisaTon with the currently recommended lower Hb targets for people with CKD.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For people with symptomaTc anaemia associated with non-dialysis dependent chronic kidney disease at the Tme of treatment iniTaTon, HIF-PHI therapy is recommended by NICE (5) as a treatment opTon and is comparable to ESA therapy. NICE recommends that the choice between a HIF-PHI and ESA should be made in discussion with the individual.
texts.text: For people with symptomaTc anaemia associated with non-dialysis dependent chronic kidney disease at the Tme of treatment iniTaTon, HIF-PHI therapy is recommended by NICE (5) as a treatment opTon and is comparable to ESA therapy. NICE recommends that the choice between a HIF-PHI and ESA should be made in discussion with the individual.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In determining target Hb guidelines it is important to assess potenTal benefits (such as possible improved survival, improvement in health-related quality of life (HRQoL) and avoidance of transfusion requirement and hospitalisaTon) vs. potenTal harms (increased mortality and vascular events) and paTent choice (6, 7) .
texts.text: In determining target Hb guidelines it is important to assess potenTal benefits (such as possible improved survival, improvement in health-related quality of life (HRQoL) and avoidance of transfusion requirement and hospitalisaTon) vs. potenTal harms (increased mortality and vascular events) and paTent choice (6, 7) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The randomised open-label phase 3 ESA-controlled DOLOMITES trial in NDD-CKD, included 28 countries and the UK reported that the safety of the HIF-PHI was comparable to the ESA's in the majority of safety variables in people with NDD-CKD (8) . They also reported no evidence of increased cardiovascular safety or mortality risk when compared with ESA's (8) The study included NDD-CKD people with symptomaTc anaemia and stage 3, 4 or 5 CKD with Hb levels <105 g/L at the start of treatment. NICE commiXee recommends treatment at a Hb of less than 100 g/L. (5) .
texts.text: The randomised open-label phase 3 ESA-controlled DOLOMITES trial in NDD-CKD, included 28 countries and the UK reported that the safety of the HIF-PHI was comparable to the ESA's in the majority of safety variables in people with NDD-CKD (8) . They also reported no evidence of increased cardiovascular safety or mortality risk when compared with ESA's (8) The study included NDD-CKD people with symptomaTc anaemia and stage 3, 4 or 5 CKD with Hb levels <105 g/L at the start of treatment. NICE commiXee recommends treatment at a Hb of less than 100 g/L. (5) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The NICE CommiXee reported that HIF-PHI therapy was non inferior to ESA therapy in all measures of quality of Life and that the DOLOMITES trial demonstrated that people with NDD-CKD required less IV iron than those treated with an ESA.
texts.text: The NICE CommiXee reported that HIF-PHI therapy was non inferior to ESA therapy in all measures of quality of Life and that the DOLOMITES trial demonstrated that people with NDD-CKD required less IV iron than those treated with an ESA.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the case of non-responsiveness, treatment with HIF-PHI should not be conTnued beyond 24 weeks aVer the start of treatment and other causes of anaemia be invesTgated (9) .
texts.text: In the case of non-responsiveness, treatment with HIF-PHI should not be conTnued beyond 24 weeks aVer the start of treatment and other causes of anaemia be invesTgated (9) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Meta analysis of RCTS comparing HIF-PHI with ESA therapy have also demonstrated that there was no significant difference in adjudicated MACE events (10 trials) (1.00, 95% CI 0.94-1.07). For MACE+ again no difference was seen between HIF-PHI and ESA therapy (1.01, 95% CI 0.95-1.06) even aVer sensiTvity analysis (10) . There was also no difference in the mortality rate, but one must be cauToned as the duraTon of follow up was relaTvely short.

texts.text: Rationale for Guidelines 4.4-4.6

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Currently only 1 HIF-PHI has been recommended in the UK by NICE for people who have stage 3 to 5 CKD with no iron deficiency and are not on dialysis at the start of HIF-PHI treatment. Compared with ESA these drugs work as well for anaemia management (1, 2) . NICE has therefore recommended that HIF-PHI can be used as an alternaTve to ESA for people with NDD-CKD stages 3 to 5, especially if they find self-injecTng difficult or painful or rely on others to give them their injecTons. StarTng doses are based on weight, and dose changes are based on response to treatment and changes in Hb levels. The correcTon phase lasts up to approximately 3 months from starTng treatment or unTl a stable Hb level of 100-120 g/L is achieved and stabilised. The maintenance phase starts immediately aVer the correcTon phase. Clinicians with experTse in this field have indicated that the decision whether to stop treatment depends on how well anaemia is managed. They stated that they would Ttrate the dose down if Hb levels reached approximately 125 g/L so that Hb levels stay within a 'presumed safe range' (that is, between 100 g/L and 120 g/L) rather than stopping treatment completely.

StarTng doses are based on weight, and dose changes are based on response to treatment and changes in Hb levels. The correcTon phase lasts up to approximately 3 months from starTng treatment or unTl a stable Hb level of 100-120 g/L is achieved and stabilised. The maintenance phase starts immediately aVer the correcTon phase. Clinicians with experTse in this field have indicated that the decision whether to stop treatment depends on how well anaemia is managed. They stated that they would Ttrate the dose down if Hb levels reached approximately 125 g/L so that Hb levels stay within a 'presumed safe range' (that is, between 100 g/L and 120 g/L) rather than stopping treatment completely.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Adjustments to HIF-PHI doses should be considered when Hb is <105 or >115 g/L in adults to balance the benefit and safety to people given the current evidence base. These thresholds for intervenTon should achieve a populaTon distribuTon centred on a mean of 110 g/L with a range of 100-120 g/L, similar to ESA based therapy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The intermiXent dosing strategy with HIF-PHI for the treatment of anaemia in people with chronic kidney disease was developed to maintain its effecTveness. This is the case for Roxadustat with a half life of 15 hours administered three Tmes per week, enabling HIF transcripTonal acTvity to return to baseline between doses, which results in the intermiXent inducTon of hypoxia-inducible target genes involved in erythropoiesis. However, for vadadustat which has a half-life of approximately 4.5 - 9.2 hours it is given daily.

texts.orig: Malignancy
texts.text: Malignancy

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hypoxia inducible factor-1 alpha (HIF-1α) is a key transcripTon factor in tumour progression (1) . HIF1α inhibitors are currently used in cancer treatment (1) .  GeneTc HIF acTvaTon is a central mechanism of tumorigenesis in people with the von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease and clear cell renal carcinomas (2) . These data suggest that HIF-PHI may potenTally increase the risk of iniTaTon/ progression of malignancy in people with CKD.  Data from the most recent metanalysis of over 24,000 people indicate the risk esTmate for malignancy was 7% lower in the HIF-PHI arm compared to ESA (nonsignificant RR 0.93; 95% CI 0.76 to 1.13) (3) . However, once again follow-up was relaTvely short- and longer-term data is needed to assess for the development of new cancers and impact on previous cancers. Those with established cancer or a history of cancer were excluded from all trials.
texts.text: Hypoxia inducible factor-1 alpha (HIF-1α) is a key transcripTon factor in tumour progression (1) . HIF1α inhibitors are currently used in cancer treatment (1) .  GeneTc HIF acTvaTon is a central mechanism of tumorigenesis in people with the von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease and clear cell renal carcinomas (2) . These data suggest that HIF-PHI may potenTally increase the risk of iniTaTon/ progression of malignancy in people with CKD.  Data from the most recent metanalysis of over 24,000 people indicate the risk esTmate for malignancy was 7% lower in the HIF-PHI arm compared to ESA (nonsignificant RR 0.93; 95% CI 0.76 to 1.13) (3) . However, once again follow-up was relaTvely short- and longer-term data is needed to assess for the development of new cancers and impact on previous cancers. Those with established cancer or a history of cancer were excluded from all trials.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the ASCEND-ND trial, cancer-related death or tumour progression or recurrence was numerically more common in those randomised to daprodustat (72 of 1937, 3.7%) versus darbepoeTn alfa (49 of 1933, 2.5%), with a relaTve risk of 1.47 (95% CI: 1.03- 2.10) (4) . Post hoc analyses that accounted for differenTal dosing frequency aXenuated this observed risk (4) . Molidustat in clinical trials also reported neoplasms in 9.8% of trial parTcipants in the molidustat group compared with 5.3% in the darbepoeTn group (5) .
texts.text: In the ASCEND-ND trial, cancer-related death or tumour progression or recurrence was numerically more common in those randomised to daprodustat (72 of 1937, 3.7%) versus darbepoeTn alfa (49 of 1933, 2.5%), with a relaTve risk of 1.47 (95% CI: 1.03- 2.10) (4) . Post hoc analyses that accounted for differenTal dosing frequency aXenuated this observed risk (4) . Molidustat in clinical trials also reported neoplasms in 9.8% of trial parTcipants in the molidustat group compared with 5.3% in the darbepoeTn group (5) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There has been no consistent signal across the published HIF-PHI trials of an excess risk of malignancy-related adverse events, but since paTents with a history of recent or acTve malignancy were excluded from trials, it is difficult to conclude with confidence of the absence of a clinically relevant risk of the use of these drugs compared with ESAs. It is therefore advisable that the use of this product should be avoided or used cauTously in people with known acTve malignancy.
texts.text: There has been no consistent signal across the published HIF-PHI trials of an excess risk of malignancy-related adverse events, but since paTents with a history of recent or acTve malignancy were excluded from trials, it is difficult to conclude with confidence of the absence of a clinically relevant risk of the use of these drugs compared with ESAs. It is therefore advisable that the use of this product should be avoided or used cauTously in people with known acTve malignancy.

texts.text: Polycys9c Kidney Disease

texts.label: text
texts.orig: People with CKD who have underlying PolycysTc Kidney Disease were excluded from trials using HIF PHI's because of the potenTal risk of malignant transformaTon of cysts. HIF acTvaTon occurs in polycysTc kidneys in humans and rodents and acTvaTon of the HIF-pathway has been shown to enhance cyst expansion in preclinical models. However, whether the use of HIF-PHI therapy to treat anaemia may enhance cyst growth remains to be further clarified. It has been postulated that cyst formaTon is related to chloride-dependent fluid secreTon into the cyst lumen (6) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Pre-clinical studies in healthy rats and rats with CKD demonstrated that HIF-PHI therapy generate significant dose-dependent blood-pressure lowering effects (7, 8) . However, so far, no significant blood pressure safety concerns have been reported in any HIF-PHI phase 3 programs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DiabeTc reTnopathy may be exacerbated potenTally by the effect of HIF-PHI therapy on neovascularisaTon (9) . There were few parTcipants with significant reTnopathy recruited to the current published phase 3 clinical trials. Data from a pooled Japanese analysis of trials in NDD-CKD and DDCKD using daprodustat did not report any increased risk for reTnal events or deterioraTon in disease (10) . One relaTvely large study, the SYMPHONY-ND study which compared enarodustat ESA therapy did show an increase in the levels of VEGF levels and an increase in reported reTnal adverse events (3.7 vs 0.9%) (11) . Other NDD-CKD trials have not shown increased risk of eye disease with HIF-PHI therapy (12, 13, 14, 15) .

Roxadustat showed an excess risk of thrombosis in both NDD-CKD (versus ESA) and DD-CKD (versus placebo) trials (22) . Vascular access thrombosis occurred in 1.5 versus 0.9/100 paTents years and DVT was 0.7 versus 0.2/100 paTents years (23) . A pooled analysis of roxadustat trials demonstrated higher risks were associated with the rate of Hb rise (24) . Lower doses of roxadustat, may lead to a slower rate of Hb rise, and thus ameliorate thrombosis risk while maintaining efficacy. ThromboTc risk with vadadustat were not noted in published data (25) . Daprodustat trials have not reported a significant excess risk of thrombosis compared with acTve comparator although there was a numerical increased risk of vascular access thrombosis (2.1% versus 1.5%) (4, 25, 26) .

texts.text: Pa9ents hyporesponsive to ESAs

texts.label: text
texts.orig: By lowering hepcidin levels, HIF-PHI therapy may theoreTcally be more effecTve in treaTng people who are hyporesponsive to ESAs because of chronic inflammaTon or funcTonal iron deficiency. Preliminary data from the randomised trials suggest that whereas higher doses of ESAs are needed for people with high C-reacTve protein (CRP) levels, this may not be true for HIF-PHI therapy. (27, 28) However, CRP concentraTons that were considered high in trial parTcipants were only slightly elevated, and sicker and more inflamed paTents may have been less likely to have been enrolled in trials of HIF-PHI therapy. Therefore, more evidence from randomised controlled trials are needed before recommendaTons for rouTne use in the populaTon can be made.

texts.orig: Although the use of HIF-PHI therapy in combinaTon with ESAs might theoreTcally be advantageous for people who are ESA hyporesponsive, there are no data available to support this strategy in clinical pracTce at present (29) . As with all drugs, there is a risk for drug-drug interacTons with the use of HIFPHI therapy, parTcularly in combinaTon with other oral agents.

texts.orig: For MACE outcomes, all trials with a HIF-PHI met the non-inferiority margin set by the regulator except Vadadustat in people with NDD-CKD. However, there is bias in these trials due to the withdrawal of large number of people in both arms (over 20%). A recent meta-analysis has shown no difference as described previously (3) . A cochrane meta-analysis of 51 trials with almost 31,000 parTcipants with NDD-CKD or DD-CKD found no difference in CV death (RR 1.05: 95% CI 0.88 to 1.26); non-fatal MI (RR 0.91: 95% CI 0.76 to 1.00 or non-fatal stroke (RR 1.06: 95% CI 0.71 to 1.56) (30).  The secondary outcome studied in the DOLOMITES trial was the impact of roxadustat compared to the acTve comparator (darbepoeTn alfa) on CV safety, based on the number of major adverse cardiac events (26, 27) . texts.label: text
texts.orig: Roxadustat was previous reviewed by the FDA for its potenTal use in CKD (29, 31) . Data submiXed included 3 separate trials comparing Roxadustat with placebo that were pooled for meta-analyses in the NDD-CKD (n = 4270) populaTon (32) . A fourth study comparing roxadustat to darbepoeTn alfa was analysed separately (DOLOMITES) (33) . The pooled analyses for roxadustat did not have prespecified agreed (FDA) noninferiority margins (32, 33) . Further this risk was accentuated during on-treatment sensiTvity analyses (as opposed to intenTon-to-treat analysis). This analysis was suggested by the FDA to minimise the effect of including unexposed person-Tmes or events that may not be affected by the intervenTon (18) . When assessing events occurring while people were on treatment and for one week aVer disconTnuaTon (on-treatment + 7 days analyses), 277 (7.2%) events were recorded in the roxadustat arm compared with 131 (5.6%) events in the placebo arm (HR 1.38; 95% CI: 1.111.70). However, there was higher dropout rates in the placebo compared to roxadustat arms, with potenTal for bias that may have disadvantaged roxadustat (33) .

Data submiXed included 3 separate trials comparing Roxadustat with placebo that were pooled for meta-analyses in the NDD-CKD (n = 4270) populaTon (32) . A fourth study comparing roxadustat to darbepoeTn alfa was analysed separately (DOLOMITES) (33) . This analysis was suggested by the FDA to minimise the effect of including unexposed person-Tmes or events that may not be affected by the intervenTon (18) . When assessing events occurring while people were on treatment and for one week aVer disconTnuaTon (on-treatment + 7 days analyses), 277 (7.2%) events were recorded in the roxadustat arm compared with 131 (5.6%) events in the placebo arm (HR 1.38; 95% CI: 1.111.70). texts.label: text
texts.orig: Three studies of roxadustat compared to ESA involving people receiving dialysis (N=3880) were metaanalysed (33) . The analyses of the effect of roxadustat for MACE were discordant based on the analyTcal approach: in the primary, on-treatment + 7-day analyses, the risk of MACE was similar in the roxadustat and ESA groups: HR 1.02; 95% CI: 0.88-1.20. In the sensiTvity, on-treatment analysis, the HR for the risk of MACE in paTents treated with roxadustat versus ESA was 1.14; 95% CI: 1.001.30 (not staTsTcal significance for non-inferiority). A fourth trial conducted in Europe and not included in the pooled meta-analysis, permiXed the use of two different ESAs (epoeTn alfa or darbepoeTn alfa) as comparators, demonstrated a higher risk of death in roxadustat vs. ESA-treated paTents (8.9 per 100 paTent years (PY) vs. 6.3 per 100 PY; HR 1.54, 95% CI 1.04-2.28) (29) .

texts.orig: The differences observed in the stable DD populaTon maybe somewhat challenging to accurately interpret due to the different treatment strategies for the Roxadustat and the comparator arm. While in the comparator arm in the stable dialysis dependent CKD (SDD) studies the treatment was not changed (paTents conTnued ESA therapy) in the Roxadustat arm were switched from ESA therapy with inherent risks related to this change which created an imbalance between the two arms (35) . Several contribuTng factors may impact this risk, including treatment non-responsiveness, and converTng stable ESA treated people receiving dialysis.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the case of non-responsiveness, treatment with roxadustat should not be conTnued beyond 24 weeks aVer the start of treatment. Conversion of people receiving dialysis otherwise stable on ESA treatment is only to be considered when there is a valid clinical reason and aVer a shared decision with the paTent on the risks and benefits. For stable ESA treated people with anaemia associated with CKD and not on dialysis, this risk could not be esTmated as these paTents have not been studied. A decision to treat these paTents with roxadustat should be based on a shared decision with the paTent of the benefits and risks for that parTcular individual paTent.'

texts.orig: In a published analysis of the four roxadustat trials in the DD-CKD populaTon (8) , MACE and MACE+  in the on-treatment plus 7 day analyses showed different results in incident vs. prevalent dialysis paTents, noted similar risk of MACE (HR 0.83; 95% CI: 0.61-1.13) and nominally lower risk of MACE+ (HR 0.76; 95% CI: 0.57-1.00) among incident dialysis paTents treated with roxadustat, whereas roxadustat was less favourable for MACE (HR 1.18; 95% CI: 1.00-1.38) and all-cause mortality (HR 1.23; 95% CI: 1.02-1.49) in prevalent dialysis paTents (33) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Pooled results from two randomized trials involving NDD-CKD persons in which vadadustat was compared with darbepoeTn alfa were analysed (19) .  One trial involved ESA naïve paTents (n=1751) and the other involved paTents who were receiving acTve ESA therapy (n =1725). Pooled analysis showed that while vadadustat met its margin with respect to hematologic efficacy in each trial, it did not meet its pre-specified non inferiority margin (HR 1.3) with respect to MACE, defined as death from any cause, non-fatal myocardial infarcTon, or non-fatal stroke, with a higher risk of MACE in the vadadustat arm (25) . The excess risk was accounted for by non-fatal MI and death from noncardiovascular causes. Subgroup analyses found a regional difference in the study results, with the increased MACE risk observed in non-U.S. study sites (HR 1.30; 95% CI 1.05-1.62) but no difference in risk in the U.S. study sites (HR 1.06; 95% CI 0.87-1.29).

texts.orig: Two randomised trials involving DD-CKD paTents compared vadadustat with darbepoeTn alfa.  One trial involved prevalent (n = 3554) and the other incident (n = 369) parTcipants (34) . Pooled analysis of results from both trials showed similar MACE rates in the two arms and met non-inferiority (HR 0.96; 95% CI: 0.83-1.11) (34) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A phase 3 trial assessed the safety and efficacy of daprodustat against an acTve comparator (darbepoeTn alfa) for the treatment in people with NDD-CKD. Daprodustat was non-inferior to the acTve comparator as it met the pre-specified non-inferiority margins of a HR of 1.25 in primary analyses (HR: 1.03; 95% CI: 0.89-1.19) (24) . However, in the sensiTvity on-treatment MACE analysis, which censored paTents at 28 days aVer the last dose, parTcipants randomised to daprodustat had a higher incidence of MACE than those randomised to ESA (14.1% vs. 10.5%, HR 1.40; 95% CI: 1.171.68).  The trial authors suggested that differences in the dosing frequency of daprodustat versus ESAs in this trial and differences in definiTons of treatment periods may have led to potenTal bias that disadvantaged daprodustat (24) .
texts.text: A phase 3 trial assessed the safety and efficacy of daprodustat against an acTve comparator (darbepoeTn alfa) for the treatment in people with NDD-CKD. Daprodustat was non-inferior to the acTve comparator as it met the pre-specified non-inferiority margins of a HR of 1.25 in primary analyses (HR: 1.03; 95% CI: 0.89-1.19) (24) . However, in the sensiTvity on-treatment MACE analysis, which censored paTents at 28 days aVer the last dose, parTcipants randomised to daprodustat had a higher incidence of MACE than those randomised to ESA (14.1% vs. 10.5%, HR 1.40; 95% CI: 1.171.68).  The trial authors suggested that differences in the dosing frequency of daprodustat versus ESAs in this trial and differences in definiTons of treatment periods may have led to potenTal bias that disadvantaged daprodustat (24) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Daprodustat was evaluated in randomised, open-label, phase 3 trial, in paTents with DD-CKD subjects using the acTve comparator of epoeTn alfa if they were receiving haemodialysis or darbepoeTn alfa if they were receiving peritoneal dialysis (5) . A total of 2964 paTents underwent randomizaTon. Daprodustat, compared to the acTve comparators, met the pre-specified noninferiority margin of 1.25 in primary analyses of the DD-CKD populaTons (DD: 0.93; 95% CI: 0.811.07). Similar conclusions were obtained from sensiTvity analysis.

texts.orig: Overall meta-analyses have reassured the short-term safety of these drugs in comparison to ESA therapy. Natale et al (30) found not difference in MACE and MACE+; similar to those from Takkavatakarn et al (37) . A meta-analysis limited to only non-dialysis and acTve comparator (9 studies with 9470 people) there was no difference in CV outcomes (3) .

A meta-analysis limited to only non-dialysis and acTve comparator (9 studies with 9470 people) there was no difference in CV outcomes (3) .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: More long-term data will be needed to reassure on the impact of HIF-PHI therapy and their potenTal off-target effects including the systemic response to their acTon via inhibiTon of alpha ketoglutarate which may have effects of cardiovascular and heart failure complicaTons (38) . Several studies are ongoning including comparing roxadustat combined with sacubitril/valsartan versus recombinant human erythropoieTn combined with ACEI or ARB in Chinese paTents with cardiorenal syndrome and anaemia (NCT05053893) and examining the safety and efficacy of roxadustat in the treatment of HF in paTents with CKD and anaemia (NCT05691257).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Treatment of Anaemia of CKD with Blood Transfusions (Guidelines 5.1 - 5.1.3)
texts.text: 5. Treatment of Anaemia of CKD with Blood Transfusions (Guidelines 5.1 - 5.1.3)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Guideline 5.1 Blood Transfusion in People with Anaemia of CKD
texts.text: Guideline 5.1 Blood Transfusion in People with Anaemia of CKD

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We recommend that in people with anaemia of CKD, especially those in whom kidney transplantaTon is an opTon, red blood cell transfusion should be avoided, if possible, to minimise the risk of allosensiTsaTon. (1A)
texts.text: We recommend that in people with anaemia of CKD, especially those in whom kidney transplantaTon is an opTon, red blood cell transfusion should be avoided, if possible, to minimise the risk of allosensiTsaTon. (1A)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Guideline 5.1.1
texts.text: Guideline 5.1.1

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We recommend if red blood cell transfusion becomes essenTal (usually in the seLng of acute blood loss, acute haemolysis or severe sepsis) transfusion should be based on policies set by local transfusion guidelines rather than Hb target range for ESA therapy in chronic anaemia of CKD. (1B)
texts.text: We recommend if red blood cell transfusion becomes essenTal (usually in the seLng of acute blood loss, acute haemolysis or severe sepsis) transfusion should be based on policies set by local transfusion guidelines rather than Hb target range for ESA therapy in chronic anaemia of CKD. (1B)
